pr.title: Fix various literals and frame sizes
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Automation-assisted matching sweep over nonmatching functions. The PR changed many source files by correcting literal candidates and adding, removing, resizing, or repositioning stack padding. The author said two scripts were used: one for incorrect literals and one for frame sizes, with only results that improved total match score retained. The decomp.dev report showed a small overall gain: matched code 66.30% (+0.01%, +212 bytes), matched data 38.71% (+16 bytes), 2 new matches, 192 unmatched-item improvements, and one gmresult .sdata2 regression.

key_files.why: Moved/added padding in camera functions and changed pause camera literals in Camera_8002F9E4: pause_eye_offset y/z 85.0f/730.0f to 5.0f/20.0f and pause_eye_distance 35.0f to 3.0f.
ftCo_800A2718 was rewritten from numeric bool returns and an OR-like condition into nested bool returns; bot report listed this as the largest single code improvement (+9 bytes, 80.42% to 82.34%).
Changed a facing-direction literal comparison from 1.0F to -1.0f in ftColl_80078C70 and added PAD_STACK(8) in ftColl_80079C70.
Added PAD_STACK in several animation helpers; decomp.dev reported grAnime_801C83D0 as a new full function match.
Changed frame threshold from >0.0f to >12.0f and added padding; decomp.dev reported the unit's .sdata2 as a new full match.
Multiple literal and frame-size fixes, including platform spawn offsets 15.0F/10.0F to 8.0f/5.0f and a 1.5707963F argument changed to 500.0f.
Largest touched file by hunks; many PAD_STACK insertions plus assert line literal corrections such as 0x4A7 to 0x47A and 2639 to 2635.
Several result-screen padding additions plus KO-time text scale literal 0.09F to 0.11f; bot report also flagged gmresult .sdata2 as the one regression.
Multiple PAD_STACK and explicit _padA changes in particle/debug rendering code; hsd_8039254C was listed among improved unmatched items.
Ten menu/stat-screen padding insertions, including large PAD_STACK values, making it one of the broader frame-size adjustments.
Eight trophy-mode padding insertions across unlock, camera/light/display, and toy setup helpers.
Literal-only baselib fix in HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC: far_val changed from 0.5F to 2.0f.

what_changed: Touched 84 source files with +212/-45 lines; no committed script files appear in the changed-file list, despite the author reporting script-assisted generation.
Added or adjusted stack padding widely through PAD_STACK(n), including small 8/16/24 byte pads and large pads such as PAD_STACK(264), PAD_STACK(328), and PAD_STACK(160).
Replaced some trailing PAD_STACK uses with explicit byte-array locals such as u8 _padA[16], u8 _padA[24], and u8 _padA[152], changing where padding appears relative to other locals.

Removed or resized some padding where the script found a better frame size, for example gmstaffroll PAD_STACK(0x60) to PAD_STACK(88), mnsnap PAD_STACK(336) to PAD_STACK(328), and sislib PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(16).

Changed literal constants across camera, fighter, stage, interface, result-screen, and baselib code, including floats, assert line numbers, OSReport line numbers, frame thresholds, and comparison constants.
Added #include "placeholder.h" in src/melee/gr/grkongo.c alongside new PAD_STACK usage in that file.
Automated scoring reported 2 new matches: main/melee/if/if_2F72 .sdata2 and main/melee/gr/granime grAnime_801C83D0.
Automated scoring reported 192 improvements in unmatched items but also 1 regression in main/melee/gm/gmresult .sdata2.
smart_moves: Used broad automation as a search tool but filtered candidates by total match score instead of blindly committing every script output.
Kept the PR narrowly focused on match mechanics: literals and frame layout, with minimal semantic restructuring outside the few changed condition/return shapes visible in the diff.
Used both PAD_STACK and explicit u8 _padA locals, which is useful when matching requires not just the frame size but also a specific local-slot ordering.
Accepted tiny per-function improvements as worthwhile in aggregate: the report shows many +1 or +2 byte improvements across unrelated modules.
Called out false positives in the PR body, setting expectations that the script results were manually curated and not inherently authoritative.
decomp_lessons: Nonmatches can be caused by very small details: a single float literal, assert line number, or stack-frame size can change code/data match scores.
Automated literal and frame-size sweeps are useful for late-stage cleanup, but they should be reviewed against both code and data match metrics.
A total score improvement can still contain local regressions; here gmresult .sdata2 regressed while the overall PR improved matched code/data.
Stack padding is a matching tactic, not necessarily final source truth. Reviewer feedback explicitly warned that some frame-size changes may need to be changed back when final matches are solved.
When PAD_STACK at the end of declarations does not produce the right layout, an explicit local byte array before other locals can better model the compiler's stack allocation.
Literal fixes should be kept searchable because later decomp work may need to distinguish true original constants from score-improving false positives.
naming_conventions: No broad rename or symbol-discovery convention was introduced; most affected functions remain existing address-derived names such as Camera_8002F9E4, ftCo_800A2718, grBigBlue_801E8A1C, and fn_80175880.
Anonymous stack filler is represented with PAD_STACK(n) when only frame size matters.
Explicit placeholder local arrays use names like _padA and _padB when padding must participate in local ordering.
Boolean-returning code in ftCo_800A2718 was changed from numeric 0/1 returns to false/true keywords.
grkongo.c added placeholder.h in the same diff that introduced new PAD_STACK uses there.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Frame-size tactic: add, delete, or resize PAD_STACK(n) to make stack frame size and prologue/epilogue code match expected assembly.
Stack-order tactic: use explicit u8 _padA[N] locals before real locals when trailing PAD_STACK does not model the desired stack layout.
Literal tactic: substitute float/int constants to affect immediate loads, fconst selection, and .sdata2 contents; examples include 0.09F to 0.11f, 0.5F to 2.0f, 17 to 16, and 0.0f to 12.0f.
Line-number literal tactic: adjust assert/OSReport line numbers where source line constants are part of the compiled output.
Control-flow shape tactic: ftCo_800A2718 changed bool-return structure, showing that a literal/branch search can surface small source-shape changes beyond raw constants.
Metric tactic: run candidate changes over all nonmatching functions and keep the subset that improves aggregate match score, then inspect any reported regressions.

review_feedback.comment: The literal changes are nice. I don't mind the stack frame changes, but I think there will be at least some instances where the final match will need to change it back to what it was before.
GALE01 report: matched code 66.30% (+0.01%, +212 bytes), matched data 38.71% (+16 bytes), 2 new matches, 192 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 gmresult .sdata2 regression.

searchable_terms: Fix various literals and frame sizes
follow_up_queries: Find PAD_STACK changes from pr-2467 that were later reverted or adjusted for final matches.
Investigate gmresult .sdata2 regression after PR 2467 and whether the 0.11f change persisted.
Compare explicit u8 _padA locals versus PAD_STACK placement in functions touched by PR 2467.
Look for the uncommitted or later-published scripts/methods used to find incorrect literals and frame sizes.
